evals.prompt: Every Monday I do the same thing: pull our backend error logs from the last 7 days, group them by service, count the top 10 recurring errors, and write a short summary for the team standup. I'm tired of redoing it from scratch each week. Can you turn this into a reusable skill?
evals.expected_output: A new skill directory whose name matches its frontmatter, with a SKILL.md that has spec-valid frontmatter and an imperative body, plus proposed test cases. The skill should be validated before handoff.
expectations: Produces a skill directory containing a SKILL.md file
The SKILL.md name field is kebab-case (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only) and matches the skill's directory name exactly
The description field states both what the skill does and when to trigger it, is 1024 characters or fewer, and contains no angle brackets
The SKILL.md body is written as imperative instructions rather than a description of the skill
Depth beyond the core steps is pushed into references/ rather than inflating SKILL.md, or the body is short enough that no split is needed
Proposes or writes at least two realistic test-case prompts (for example in evals/evals.json) for the new skill
Runs a validator (skills-ref validate or python -m scripts.quick_validate) against the new skill and reports the result

evals.prompt: I downloaded this skill but it won't load and I think the SKILL.md is malformed. Make it spec-compliant and confirm it actually validates.
evals.expected_output: Every frontmatter violation in the fixture is identified and fixed (stray top-level keys moved under metadata, name made kebab-case and matched to the directory, angle brackets removed from the description), and the bundled validator is re-run and reports the skill as valid.
expectations: Identifies that the name 'Weekly_Log_Summary' is not kebab-case and does not match the directory name 'broken-skill'
Identifies that the description contains angle brackets, which are disallowed
Identifies that 'author' and 'version' are not recognized top-level frontmatter fields and belong under metadata
Fixes every identified violation so the frontmatter uses only the recognized fields with a kebab-case name matching the directory and a bracket-free description
Re-runs the validator (skills-ref validate or python -m scripts.quick_validate) and reports that the skill now validates

evals.prompt: I built a skill that turns meeting transcripts into action-item lists, but it almost never fires when I actually want it, even though that's exactly what I'm asking for. Its current description is just 'Processes meeting transcripts.' Can you rewrite the description so it triggers reliably?
evals.expected_output: An improved description selected through an eval-driven loop: a set of realistic trigger queries split should-trigger and should-not-trigger, a train/held-out split with selection on the held-out score, and a final description that is more pushy and specific while staying within the spec limits. The before and after are shown to the user.
expectations: Generates roughly 20 trigger-eval queries split between should-trigger and should-not-trigger cases
Includes near-miss should-not-trigger queries that share keywords or domain with the skill but need something different, not obviously-irrelevant negatives
Splits the queries into train and held-out sets and selects the winning description by the held-out score rather than the train score
The final description states what the skill does and when to use it, is more specific than the original, is 1024 characters or fewer, and contains no angle brackets
Shows the user the before and after descriptions with their scores
